{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "DevOps Engineer",
  "description": "WakaEat was born from a deep passion for African culinary richness and the desire to share it with the entire world. We saw a gap &mdash; Africans, the diaspora, and food enthusiasts often struggle to access authentic African meals, especially when traveling or living abroad.

WakaEat was created to close this gap, connecting people everywhere to the dishes they love and miss.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: DevOps Engineer

Job Location: Lagos (Remote) - Onsite collaboration required when requested
Job type: Full-time

Description


	We are building the first truly global African food marketplace launching simultaneously across Lagos and Washington DC.


The Mission


	Complete our AWS infrastructure setup and get all backend services fully deployed and stable. Fast. This is not a planning role. We need someone who executes.


You are the right fit if:


	You have done this before and have the receipts to prove it
	You work independently without handholding
	You deliver within tight timelines
	You communicate clearly and proactively


What You Will Deliever


	Full deployment of all backend microservices on AWS ECS
	CI/CD pipeline setup and optimization via GitHub Actions
	AWS infrastructure management including ECS, RDS, MemoryDB, Route 53 and Secrets Manager
	SSL configuration and domain management
	Environment variable configuration across all services
	Full handover documentation for internal team continuity


Requirements


	Proven hands on AWS experience especially ECS, RDS and Route 53
	Strong Docker and containerized deployment skills
	GitHub Actions or equivalent CI/CD experience
	Available to start immediately
	Fluent English


Bonus:


	Startup background
	Microservices experience
	Food tech or marketplace background


Remuneration


	Probation Period (4 Months): N300,000 / Month
	Post Probation: Salary reviewed based on performance


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a note on your previous DevOps projects, to: careers@wakaeat.com using &quot;DevOps Engineer Application &mdash; [Your Name]&quot; as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-24",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-25",

  },

DevOps Engineer Job at WakaEat




        
    

        
        WakaEat was born from a deep passion for African culinary richness and the desire to share it with the entire world. We saw a gap — Africans, the diaspora, and food enthusiasts often struggle to access authentic African meals, especially when traveling or living abroad.

WakaEat was created to close this gap, connecting people everywhere to the dishes they love and miss.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: DevOps Engineer

Job Location: Lagos (Remote) - Onsite collaboration required when requested
Job type: Full-time

Description
     

            
    




	We are building the first truly global African food marketplace launching simultaneously across Lagos and Washington DC.


The Mission


	Complete our AWS infrastructure setup and get all backend services fully deployed and stable. Fast. This is not a planning role. We need someone who executes.


You are the right fit if:


	You have done this before and have the receipts to prove it
	You work independently without handholding
	You deliver within tight timelines
	You communicate clearly and proactively


What You Will Deliever


	Full deployment of all backend microservices on AWS ECS
	CI/CD pipeline setup and optimization via GitHub Actions
	AWS infrastructure management including ECS, RDS, MemoryDB, Route 53 and Secrets Manager
	SSL configuration and domain management
	Environment variable configuration across all services
	Full handover documentation for internal team continuity


Requirements


	Proven hands on AWS experience especially ECS, RDS and Route 53
	Strong Docker and containerized deployment skills
	GitHub Actions or equivalent CI/CD experience
	Available to start immediately
	Fluent English


Bonus:


	Startup background
	Microservices experience
	Food tech or marketplace background


Remuneration


	Probation Period (4 Months): N300,000 / Month
	Post Probation: Salary reviewed based on performance


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a note on your previous DevOps projects, to: [email protected] using "DevOps Engineer Application — [Your Name]" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Capital Projects Delivery Lead",
  "description": "WTS Energy - Our client is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Capital Projects Delivery Lead
Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Our Client is seeking a Capital Projects Delivery Lead to oversee the planning, execution, and delivery of surface facility capital projects within the oil and gas sector.
	This role involves managing pipelines, processing plants, gas conditioning, produced water treatment, and waste management systems to ensure projects are completed safely, on schedule, and within budget.
	The Capital Projects Delivery Lead will coordinate multidisciplinary teams, enforce compliance with health, safety, and environmental standards, and engage with regulators, contractors, and host communities.
	Candidates can expect to lead full project lifecycles, optimize capital expenditure, and drive continuous improvement initiatives while fostering strong stakeholder relationships and ensuring operational readiness.


Responsibilities


	Lead the planning, execution, and delivery of surface facility capital projects, ensuring alignment with corporate objectives and regulatory standards
	Manage the full project lifecycle, including concept selection, FEED, engineering, procurement, construction, commissioning, and handover
	Oversee project delivery to ensure compliance with scope, cost, schedule, quality, and HSE requirements while proactively managing risks and resolving issues
	Develop and control CAPEX budgets, monitor expenditures, and implement cost control measures to optimize capital expenditure
	Lead contractor prequalification, bid evaluation, and contract award processes, ensuring adherence to local content regulations and supply chain risk mitigation
	Direct construction and commissioning activities, ensuring operational readiness and seamless handover to operations with complete documentation and training
	Promote and enforce a strong health, safety, security, and environmental culture, ensuring compliance with relevant environmental regulations and achieving Goal Zero
	Engage and coordinate with regulators, joint-venture partners, host communities, and internal stakeholders to secure necessary permits and maintain positive relations
	Identify and manage technical, commercial, and regulatory risks, implementing governance frameworks and ensuring compliance with corporate governance and audit requirements
	Lead, mentor, and develop multidisciplinary project teams, fostering collaboration across engineering, operations, finance, and commercial functions to drive performance and capability building


Qualifications
Required Qualifications:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Engineering from a recognized institution
	Minimum of 15 years of experience in oil and gas operations, including at least 5 years in a project leadership role overseeing capital projects
	Proven expertise in managing the full project lifecycle of surface facility capital projects, including pipelines, processing plants, gas conditioning, produced water treatment, and waste management systems
",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-24",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-25",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "WTS Energy"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "200904"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }
  }
}



Capital Projects Delivery Lead Job at WTS Energy




        
    

        
        WTS Energy - Our client is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Capital Projects Delivery Lead
Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	Our Client is seeking a Capital Projects Delivery Lead to oversee the planning, execution, and delivery of surface facility capital projects within the oil and gas sector.
	This role involves managing pipelines, processing plants, gas conditioning, produced water treatment, and waste management systems to ensure projects are completed safely, on schedule, and within budget.
	The Capital Projects Delivery Lead will coordinate multidisciplinary teams, enforce compliance with health, safety, and environmental standards, and engage with regulators, contractors, and host communities.
	Candidates can expect to lead full project lifecycles, optimize capital expenditure, and drive continuous improvement initiatives while fostering strong stakeholder relationships and ensuring operational readiness.


Responsibilities


	Lead the planning, execution, and delivery of surface facility capital projects, ensuring alignment with corporate objectives and regulatory standards
	Manage the full project lifecycle, including concept selection, FEED, engineering, procurement, construction, commissioning, and handover
	Oversee project delivery to ensure compliance with scope, cost, schedule, quality, and HSE requirements while proactively managing risks and resolving issues
	Develop and control CAPEX budgets, monitor expenditures, and implement cost control measures to optimize capital expenditure
	Lead contractor prequalification, bid evaluation, and contract award processes, ensuring adherence to local content regulations and supply chain risk mitigation
	Direct construction and commissioning activities, ensuring operational readiness and seamless handover to operations with complete documentation and training
	Promote and enforce a strong health, safety, security, and environmental culture, ensuring compliance with relevant environmental regulations and achieving Goal Zero
	Engage and coordinate with regulators, joint-venture partners, host communities, and internal stakeholders to secure necessary permits and maintain positive relations
	Identify and manage technical, commercial, and regulatory risks, implementing governance frameworks and ensuring compliance with corporate governance and audit requirements
	Lead, mentor, and develop multidisciplinary project teams, fostering collaboration across engineering, operations, finance, and commercial functions to drive performance and capability building


Qualifications
Required Qualifications:


	Bachelor’s degree in Engineering from a recognized institution
	Minimum of 15 years of experience in oil and gas operations, including at least 5 years in a project leadership role overseeing capital projects
	Proven expertise in managing the full project lifecycle of surface facility capital projects, including pipelines, processing plants, gas conditioning, produced water treatment, and waste management systems
    

    

        Click here to Apply

	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and CRM software.

	Career growth and professional development opportunities.

Customer Service Officer Job at Leisure Court Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Leisure court Nigeria Limited is a Nigerian real-estate development and property company that operates primarily in Abuja, Lagos and Akure. It’s a privately held firm in the housing and construction sector focused on real estate development, property sales, building services and infrastructure projects.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	As the first point of contact for our clients, you will play a vital role in creating memorable customer experiences, managing inquiries, supporting property transactions, and ensuring client satisfaction at every stage of their journey.


Key Responsibilities


	Attend to client inquiries through phone calls, emails, social media, and walk - in visits.
	Provide accurate information about available properties, payment plans, and company services.
	Resolve customer complaints promptly and professionally.
	Maintain strong relationships with clients and ensure excellent customer satisfaction.
	Keep accurate records of customer interactions and update CRM systems.
	Coordinate property inspections, site visits, and client appointments.
	Follow up on leads, prospects, and existing clients.
	Collaborate with the Sales, Marketing, and Operations teams to ensure seamless service delivery.
	Prepare customer service reports and client feedback summaries.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Business Administration, Marketing, Mass Communication, or a related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in customer service, preferably within the real estate industry.
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Strong interpersonal and relationship management abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and CRM software.
	Ability to multitask, work under pressure, and maintain professionalism at all times.
	Strong problem-solving and customer engagement skills.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary and performance incentives.
	Career growth and professional development opportunities.
	Supportive and collaborative work environment.
	Opportunity to work with a reputable and fast - growing real estate company.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Branch Manager",
  "description": "Citygate Global Investment Limited is an organization founded in June 2007 to engage in Finance business (Microfinance Finance) and registered with Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC). Citygate Global is a registered member of Association of Non-Bank Microfinance Institution of Nigeria (AMFIN) and also a registered Money Lending Company in all the States where we operate.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Branch Manager

Job Locations: Lagos and Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities&nbsp;


	Own revenue expansion within existing accounts through structured upsell and cross-sell initiatives
	Act as the commercial lead for key clients, strengthening long-term value and positioning CitygateGlobalas a core financial partner
	Build and convert a high-quality pipeline across SMEs and other products.sOwn the full sales cycle; sourcing, qualification, negotiation, and convert demand into revenue
	Proactively identify churn risks and take decisive action to protect and grow revenue
	Deliver a clear commercial growth strategy across acquisition, retention, and revenue expansion
	Be directly accountable for revenue performance across all products, with a strong focus on measurable outcomes
	Translate strategy into execution by driving pipeline generation, conversion, and revenue realization.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send application to: careers@citygateglobal.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Branch Manager Job at Citygate Global Investment Limited




        
    

        
        Citygate Global Investment Limited is an organization founded in June 2007 to engage in Finance business (Microfinance Finance) and registered with Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC). Citygate Global is a registered member of Association of Non-Bank Microfinance Institution of Nigeria (AMFIN) and also a registered Money Lending Company in all the States where we operate.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Branch Manager

Job Locations: Lagos and Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities 
     

            
    




	Own revenue expansion within existing accounts through structured upsell and cross-sell initiatives
	Act as the commercial lead for key clients, strengthening long-term value and positioning CitygateGlobalas a core financial partner
	Build and convert a high-quality pipeline across SMEs and other products.sOwn the full sales cycle; sourcing, qualification, negotiation, and convert demand into revenue
	Proactively identify churn risks and take decisive action to protect and grow revenue
	Deliver a clear commercial growth strategy across acquisition, retention, and revenue expansion
	Be directly accountable for revenue performance across all products, with a strong focus on measurable outcomes
	Translate strategy into execution by driving pipeline generation, conversion, and revenue realization.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send application to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply
